output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Step 1: Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);

    // Step 2: Get observer graph instance
    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Step 3: Add nodes to the graph (if not auto-registered)
    graph->addNode(observer);
    graph->addNode(target);

    // Step 4: Add observer relationship (observer observes target)
    graph->addOneObserver(target, observer);

    // Step 5: Notify target of change
    target->valueChanged();

    // Step 6: Return notification status
    return std::make_pair(observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount());
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <atomic>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test thread manager registration and safety check
 *
 * @param enableSafety Whether to enable thread safety
 * @return bool Whether thread safety is enabled after operations
 */
bool test_thread_manager_registration(bool enableSafety) {
    auto& thread_manager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    thread_manager.registerThread(std::this_thread::get_id());
    
    if (enableSafety) {
        thread_manager.enableThreadSafety();
    }
    
    return thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test atomic operations on reactive variables
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value for the reactive variable
 * @param addValue Value to add atomically
 * @return int The final value after atomic addition

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_reactive_calculation(int a, int b) {
    auto varA = reaction::createVar(a);
    auto varB = reaction::createVar(b);
    auto calc = reaction::createCalcExpr(
        [&](const reaction::NodePtr &a, const reaction::NodePtr &b) {
            return (a->get<int>() + b->get<int>()) * 2;
        },
        {varA, varB}
    );
    return calc->eval();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test observer node naming functionality
 *
 * @param name Name to set for the observer node
 * @return std::string The retrieved name from the observer graph
 */
std::string test_observer_naming(const std::string& name) {
    auto node = ObserverNode::create();
    auto graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.setName(node, name);
    return graph.getName(node);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <unordered_set>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test batch operations on observer nodes
 *
 * @return true if all batch operations succeed, false otherwise
 */
bool test_batch_operations() {
    // Step 1: Create observer nodes and add them to the graph
    auto node1 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>("node1");
    auto node2 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>("node2");
    ObserverGraph& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addObserver(node1);
    graph.addObserver(node2);

    // Step 3: Create a batch ID
    const void* batchId = graph.createBatch();

    // Step 4: Register nodes to the batch
    graph.addOneObserverToActiveBatch(node1, batchId);
    graph.addOneObserverToActiveBatch(node2, batchId);

    // Step 5: Verify nodes are in the active batch
    auto activeNodes = graph.getActiveBatchNodes(batchId);
    if (activeNodes.size() != 2 ||
        activeNodes.find(node1) == activeNodes.end() ||
        activeNodes.find(node2) == activeNodes.end()) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 6: Unregister the batch
    graph.closeBatch(batchId);

    // Step 7: Verify nodes are no longer in the active batch
    activeNodes = graph.getActiveBatchNodes(batchId);
    if (!activeNodes.empty()) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 8: Return success status
    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <stdexcept>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test transactional observer updates
 *
 * @param nodeName Name for the node to update
 * @return true if transactional update prevented cycle, false otherwise
 */
bool test_transactional_update(const std::string& nodeName) {
    auto observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Step 1: Create observer nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>(nodeName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("target");

    // Step 3: Set up initial relationship
    observerGraph.addObserver(source, target);

    // Step 4: Notify and verify
    source->valueChanged();
    if (!target->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }

    // Step 5: Reset nodes
    source->reset();
    target->reset();

    // Step 6: Transactional update attempt
    try {
        // Adding reverse observer to create cycle
        observerGraph.addObserverTransactional(target, source);
        return false; // Shouldn't reach here
    } catch (const std::runtime_error&) {
        // Cycle detected - expected
    } catch (const std::exception&) {
        // Other exceptions - unexpected
        return false;
    }

    // Step 8: Verify original state preserved
    source->valueChanged();
    if (target->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    // Create target node
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);

    // Create observer nodes
    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        auto obs = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name);
        observers.push_back(obs);

    // Get observer graph instance

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    // Create target node
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);

    // Create observer nodes
    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        auto observer = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name);
        observers.push_back(observer);
    }

    // Reset observers to ensure clean state
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        observer->reset();
    }

    // Get observer graph instance
    auto graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Add observers to watch the target
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        graph->addObserver(target, observer);
    }

    // Trigger notifications
    target->notify();

    // Verify notifications
    int totalCalls = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        totalCalls += observer->getCallCount();
        if (observer->getCallCount() == 0) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }

    return std::make_pair(totalCalls, allNotified);
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<int, bool> test_chained_observers(const std::string& targetName, 
                                           const std::string& observer1Name, 
                                           const std::string& observer2Name) {
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(targetName);
    auto observer1 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer1Name);
    auto observer2 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer2Name);

    auto graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph->addObCbNoNotify(target, observer1);
    graph->addObCbNoNotify(observer1, observer2);

    target->notify();

    int total = target->getCallCount() + observer1->getCallCount() + observer2->getCallCount();
    bool chainStatus = (observer1->getCallCount() > 0) && (observer2->getCallCount() > 0);
    return std::make_pair(total, chainStatus);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

    // Return the final result

} // namespace test_reaction

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_reactive_update_chain(int a, int b, int newA) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto varA = reaction::createVar<int>(a);
    auto varB = reaction::createVar<int>(b);

    // Create first calculation (sum of varA and varB)
    auto firstCalc = reaction::createCalc<int>();
    firstCalc->setOp([=](const reaction::NodePtr &node) {
        return varA->get() + varB->get();
    });
    firstCalc->addObCb(varA, reaction::ChangeTrig());
    firstCalc->addObCb(varB, reaction::ChangeTrig());

    // Create second calculation (double the first calculation)
    auto secondCalc = reaction::createCalc<int>();
    secondCalc->setOp([=](const reaction::NodePtr &node) {
        return firstCalc->get() * 2;
    });
    secondCalc->addObCb(firstCalc, reaction::ChangeTrig());

    // Update varA to trigger downstream updates
    varA->set(newA);

    // Return the final result of the second calculation
    return secondCalc->get();
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================
class PersonRecord : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age)
        : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}

    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name.getPtr()->getValue(); }
    int getAge() const { return m_age.getPtr()->getValue(); }

    void setName(const std::string& name) { m_name = name; }
    void setAge(int age) { m_age = age; }

private:
    reaction::Var<std::string> m_name;
    reaction::Var<int> m_age;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================
std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    PersonRecord pr(initialName, initialAge);
    auto calc = reaction::make_calc([](const std::string& name, int age) {
        return "Name: " + name + ", Age: " + std::to_string(age);
    }, pr.m_name, pr.m_age);
    pr.setName(newName);
    pr.setAge(newAge);
    return calc->getPtr()->getValue();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

Then in the batch, we set a to batchA and b to batchB. So after the batch, sum is batchA + batchB.         return a.get() + b.get();

        count.set(count.get() + 1);

    b.set(nonBatchB); // triggers count to 2
    int nonBatchCount = count.get();

    // Batch updates

        reaction::Batch batch;
        a.set(batchA); // no trigger
        b.set(batchB); // no trigger

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                           int initialB,
                                           int nonBatchA,
                                           int nonBatchB,
                                           int batchA,
                                           int batchB) {
    reaction::Var<int> a(initialA);
    reaction::Var<int> b(initialB);
    reaction::Var<int> count(0);

    reaction::Calc<int> sum([this](const reaction::Var<int>& a, const reaction::Var<int>& b) {
        return a.get() + b.get();
    });

    sum.addObCb([this](const int& newValue, const int& oldValue) {
        count.set(count.get() + 1);
    });

    a.set(nonBatchA);
    b.set(nonBatchB);
    int nonBatchCount = count.get();

    {
        reaction::Batch batch;
        a.set(batchA);
        b.set(batchB);
    }
    int batchCount = count.get();

    return std::make_tuple(sum.get(), nonBatchCount, batchCount);
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <sstream>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test naming reactive nodes and retrieving names
 *
 * @param valueA Initial value for varA
 * @param valueB Initial value for varB
 * @return std::string Combined string of retrieved names
 */
std::string test_named_reactions(int valueA, int valueB) {
    // Create reactive variables with names
    reaction::Var varA(valueA, "varA");
    reaction::Var varB(valueB, "varB");
    
    // Create a calculation with name
    reaction::Calc calcC([=]() {
        return varA.get() + varB.get();
    }, "calcC");
    
    // Create an action with name
    reaction::Action actionX([=]() {}, "actionX");
    
    // Retrieve names
    std::string combinedNames = varA.getName() + "," + varB.getName() + "," + calcC.getName() + "," + actionX.getName();
    return combinedNames;
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

instruction:
Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_filter_trigger` function.

The function should:
1. Create reactive variable
2. Create variables to track recomputation count and last processed value
3. Create filter-triggered calculation
4. Apply filter
5. Update sensor with first value
6. Record recompute count after first update
7. Update sensor with second value
8. Record recompute count after second update
9. Get final status
10. Return final status and counts

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test filter trigger with conditional recomputation
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial sensor value
 * @param firstUpdate First update (expected to be within threshold)
 * @param secondUpdate Second update (expected to exceed threshold)
 * @param diffThreshold Minimum delta to trigger recomputation
 * @param levelThreshold Threshold to categorize HIGH vs NORMAL
 * @return std::tuple<std::string, int, int>
 *         {final status string, recompute count after first update, recompute count after second update}
 */
std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <utility>
#include <string>
#include <memory>
#include <iostream>
#include <functional>

#include <shared_mutex>
#include <vector>
#include <sstream>
#include <stdexcept>
#include <atomic>
#include <cmath>
#include <functional>
#include <iostream>
#include <mutex>
#include <set>
#include <shared_mutex>
#include <thread>
#include <vector>

namespace reaction {

// Forward declarations
class NodeBase;
template <typename Expr, typename Type>
class VarExprBase;
template <typename Op, typename L, typename R>
class BinaryOpExprBase;
template <typename Op, typename T>
class UnaryOpExprBase;
template <typename T>
struct ValueWrapper;
template <typename T>
struct VoidWrapper;

// Simplified reactive node base
class NodeBase {
public:
    virtual ~NodeBase() = default;
    virtual void valueChanged(bool changed) {}
    virtual void notify(bool changed) {
        handleChange<true>(changed);
    }
    virtual void notifyNoNotify(bool changed) {
        handleChange<false>(changed);
    }
private:
    template <bool Notify>
    void handleChange(bool changed) {
        if constexpr (Notify) {
            this->notify(change);
        }
    }
};

// Simplified reactive variable wrapper
template <typename Type>
class Var {
public:
    using value_type = Type;

    template <typename T>
    constexpr Var(T &&t) noexcept(std::is_nothrow_constructible_v<T, Type>)
        : m_weakPtr(this) {
        value(std::forward<T>(t));
    }

    Var(const Var &other) noexcept(std::is_nothrow_constructible_v<Type, const Type &>)
        : m_weakPtr(other.m_weakPtr) {
        if (auto p = m_weakPtr.lock()) [[likely]]
            m_value = p->getValue();
    }

    Var(Var &&other) noexcept(std::is_nothrow_constructible_v<Type, Type &&>>
        : m_weakPtr(std::move(other.m_weakPtr)) {
        other.m_weakPtr.reset();
    }

    Var &operator=(const Var &other) noexcept(std::is_nothrow_assignable_v<Type, const Type &>) {
        if (this != &other) {
            m_weakPtr = other.m_weakPtr;
            if (auto p = m_weakPtr.lock()) [[likely]]
                m_value = p->getValue();
        }
        return *this;
    }

    Var &operator=(Var &&other) noexcept(std::is_nothrow_assignable_v<Type, Type &&>>) {
        if (this != &other) {
            m_weakPtr = std::move(other.m_weakPtr);
            other.m_weakPtr.reset();
        }
        return *this;
    }

    bool operator<(const Var<Type> &other) const {
        return getValue() < other.getValue();
    }

    bool operator>(const Var<Type> &other) const {
        return getValue() > other.getValue();
    }

    bool operator<=(const Var<Type> &other) const {
        return getValue() <= other.getValue();
    }

    bool operator>=(const Var<Type> &other) const {
        return getValue() >= other.getValue();
    }

    bool operator==(const Var<Type> &other) const {
        return getValue() == other.getValue();
    }

    bool operator!=(const Var<Type> &other) const {
        return getValue() != other.getValue();
    }

    // Compound assignment operators
    Var<Type> &operator+=(const Type &rhs) {
        if (auto ptr = m_weakPtr.lock()) [[likely]]
            ptr->updateValue(*ptr->getValue() += rhs);
        return *this;
    }

    Var<Type> &operator-=(const Type &rhs) {
        if (auto ptr = m_weakPtr.lock()) [[likely]]
            ptr->updateValue(*ptr->getValue() -= rhs);
        return *this;
    }

    Var<Type> &operator*=(const Type &rhs) {
        if (auto ptr = m_weakPtr.lock()) [[likely]]
            ptr->updateValue(*ptr->getValue() *= rhs);
        return *this;
    }

    Var<Type> &operator/=(const Type &rhs) {
        if (auto ptr = m_weakPtr.lock()) [[likely]]
            ptr->updateValue(*ptr->getValue() /= rhs);
        return *this;
    }

    Var<Type> &operator%=(const Type &rhs) {
        if (auto ptr = m_weakPtr.lock()) [[likely]]
            ptr->updateValue(*ptr->getValue() %= rhs);
        return *this;
    }

    Var<Type> &operator&=(const Type &rhs) {
        if (auto ptr = m_weakPtr.lock()) [[likely]]
            ptr->updateValue(*ptr->getValue() &= rhs);
        return *this;
    }

    Var<Type> &operator|=(const Type &rhs) {
        if (auto ptr = m_weakPtr.lock()) [[likely]]
            ptr->updateValue(*ptr->getValue() |= rhs);
        return *this;
    }

    Var<Type> &operator^=(const Type &rhs) {
        if (auto ptr = m_weakPtr.lock()) [[likely]]
            ptr->updateValue(*ptr->getValue() ^= rhs);
        return *this;
    }

    Var<Type> &operator<<=(const Type &rhs) {
        if (auto ptr = m_weakPtr.lock()) [[likely]]
            ptr->updateValue(*ptr->getValue() <<= rhs);
        return *this;
    }

    Var<Type> &operator>>=(const Type &rhs) {
        if (auto ptr = m_weakPtr.lock()) [[likely]]
            ptr->updateValue(*ptr->getValue() >>= rhs);
        return *this;
    }

    // Increment/decrement operators
    Var<Type> &operator++() {
        if (auto ptr = m_weakPtr.lock()) [[likely]]
            ptr->updateValue(++(*ptr->getValue()));
        return *this;
    }

    Var<Type> operator++(int) {
        if (auto ptr = m_weakPtr.lock()) [[likely]]
            return ptr->updateValue(*ptr->getValue()++);
    }

    Var<Type> &operator--() {
        if (auto ptr = m_weakPtr.lock()) [[likely]]
            ptr->updateValue(--(*ptr->getValue()));
        return *this;
    }

    Var<Type> operator--(int) {
        if (auto ptr = m_weakPtr.lock()) [[likely]]
            return ptr->updateValue(*ptr->getValue--());
    }

private:
    std::weak_ptr<NodeBase<Type>> m_weakPtr;
    Type m_value;
    bool m_isChanged{true};
    mutable std::mutex m_valueMutex;
    std::shared_mutex m_sharedMutex;
};

// Simplified reactive expression template base
template <typename Expr, typename Type>
class ExprBase : public NodeBase {
public:
    using NodeBase::NodeBase;

    template <typename T>
    void updateValue(T &&t) {
        value(std::forward<T>(t));
    }

    template <typename T>
    void value(T &&t) {
        if constexpr (std::is_same_v<Type, VoidWrapper<void>>) {
            // Void type: ignore value
            m_voidValue = Void{};
        } else {
            m_value = std::forward<T>(t);
        }
        valueChanged();
    }

    /// @brief Returns the current evaluated value of the expression.
    [[nodiscard]] decltype(auto) get() const {
        if constexpr (std::is_same_v<Type, VoidWrapper<void>>) {
            return Void{};
        } else {
            return m_value;
        }
    }

    /// @brief Returns raw pointer to the stored value (for pointer-based types).
    [[nodiscard]] auto getRaw() const {
        if constexpr (std::is_same_v<Type, VoidWrapper<void>>) {
            return nullptr;
        } else {
            return m_value.getPtr().get();
        }
    }

private:
    Type m_value;
    Void m_voidValue; ///< Placeholder for void type (maintains original behavior).
};

// Optimized reactive variable node generator
#define REACTION_DEFINE_VAR_TYPE(TypeName, Type)         \
    /**                                                   \
     * @brief Optimized reactive variable node for Type.  \
     * Inherits from ExprBase to leverage expression     \
     * system optimizations.                              \
     */                                                   \
    template <typename T>                                 \
    class TypeName##VarExpr : public ExprBase<VarExprBase<TypeName, Type>, Type> { \
    public:                                               \
        using ExprBase<VarExprBase<TypeName, Type>, Type>::ExprBase; \
    };

// Void specialization to avoid storage overhead
REACTION_DEFINE_VAR_TYPE(Void, VoidWrapper<void>);

// Basic data types
REACTION_DEFINE_VAR_TYPE(Int, ValueWrapper<int>);
REACTION_DEFINE_VAR_TYPE(Double, ValueWrapper<double>);
REACTION_DEFINE_VAR_TYPE(String, ValueWrapper<std::string>);

// Cleanup macro to avoid pollution
#undef REACTION_DEFINE_VAR_TYPE

} // namespace reaction

namespace test_reaction {

using namespace reaction;

/**
 * @brief Implementation of test_filter_trigger using optimized reactive node setup.
 *
 * This function creates a reactive pipeline with:
 * - Sensor input node (Var<Double>)
 * - Filter-triggered computation node (FilterTrigOp + ChangeTrigFilter)
 * - Status output node (Var<String>)
 * - Recompute counter node (Var<Int>)
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial sensor value
 * @param firstUpdate First update to sensor
 * @param secondUpdate Second update to sensor
 * @param diffThreshold Minimum delta to trigger recomputation
 * @param levelThreshold Threshold for high/normal status
 * @return A tuple containing final status, recompute counts after each update
 */
std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(
    double initialValue,
    double firstUpdate,
    double secondUpdate,
    double diffThreshold,
    double levelThreshold) {
    
    // Create reactive nodes
    auto sensorNode = std::make_shared<Node<DoubleVarExpr>(initialValue));
    auto statusNode = std::make_shared<Node<StringVarExpr>>();
    auto countNode = std::make_shared<Node<IntVarExpr>>(0);

    // Create filter and expression
    auto filter = ChangeTrigFilter{diffThreshold};
    auto expr = IfOp{sensorNode->getVar() > levelThreshold, "HIGH"_s, "NORMAL"_s};
    auto filterTrigOp = FilterTrigOp{expr, filter};

    // Connect nodes
    statusNode->setOp(filterTrigOp);
    
    // Create recompute counter logic
    auto changeTrig = ChangeTrigOp<true>(statusNode);
    auto incrementOp = AddOp{countNode->getVar(), 1};
    auto assignOp = AssignOp{countNode->getVar(), incrementOp};
    auto trigNode = std::make_shared<Node<NoValue>>();
    trigNode->setOp(changeTrig, assignOp);

    // Update sensor values and collect results
    sensorNode->updateValue(firstUpdate);
    int countAfterFirst = countNode->getVar().get();

    sensorNode->updateValue(secondUpdate);
    int countAfterSecond = countNode->getVar().get();

    return std::make_tuple(
        statusNode->getVar().get(),
        countAfterFirst,
        countAfterSecond);
}

} // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/observer_graph.h>
#include <reaction/field_graph.h>
#include <reaction/core.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of FieldOwnerNode's valueChanged method
inline void FieldOwnerNode::valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
    m_notificationCount++;
}

// Implementation of ProfileFields constructor
inline ProfileFields::ProfileFields(int initialScore, bool initialStatus)
    : m_score(initialScore), m_active(initialStatus) {}

// Accessor methods for ProfileFields
inline reaction::Var<int>& ProfileFields::score() { return m_score; }
inline reaction::Var<bool>& ProfileFields::active() { return m_active; }

// Implementation of test_field_binding function
inline FieldBindingResult test_field_binding(int initialScore, int scoreDelta) {
    // Step 1: Create ProfileFields
    ProfileFields pf(initialScore, true);

    // Step 2: Create FieldOwnerNode
    FieldOwnerNode node;

    // Step 3: Get observer graph
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Step 4: Register node
    graph.registerNode(&node);

    // Step 5: Get field graph
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();

    // Step 6: Bind fields to owner node
    fieldGraph.bindFields(pf, &node);

    // Step 7: Update score
    pf.score() += scoreDelta;

    // Step 8: Record notification count after score update
    int notificationsAfterScore = node.getNotificationCount();

    // Step 9: Update active field
    pf.active() = !pf.active().get();

    // Step 10: Record notification count after status update
    int notificationsAfterStatus = node.getNotificationCount();

    // Step 11: Return the results
    return FieldBindingResult{
        .notificationsAfterScoreUpdate = notificationsAfterScore,
        .notificationsAfterStatusUpdate = notificationsAfterStatus
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

inline RollbackObservation test_observer_state_rollback() {

struct CreateFactoryResult {

    int squaredValueAfterUpdate;

CreateFactoryResult test_reactive_create_factory(int baseValue, int increment) {

    auto var = reaction::createVar(baseValue);
    
    // Create reactive expression (same as variable)
    auto expr = reaction::create([=]() { return var->get(); }, var);
    
    // Create reactive calculation (square of variable)

    int initialExpr = expr->get();
    
    // Update variable
    var->set(baseValue + increment);
    
    // Updated values
    int updatedExpr = expr->get();
    int squared = calc->get();
    
    return CreateFactoryResult{
        .initialExpressionValue = initialExpr,
        .updatedExpressionValue = updatedExpr,
        .squaredValueAfterUpdate = squared
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#include <memory>
#include <reaction/observer_graph.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

NamedObserverNode::NamedObserverNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}

const std::string& NamedObserverNode::name() const noexcept { return m_name; }

ObserverCollectionSummary test_observer_collection() {
    auto a = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("A");
    auto b = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("B");
    auto c = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("C");

    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.registerNode(a);
    graph.registerNode(b);
    graph.registerNode(c);

    graph.addObserver(a, b, true); // Direct relationship
    graph.addObserver(b, c, true);  // Direct relationship (transitive via B)

    reaction::NodeSet observersSet;
    graph.collectObservers(a, observersSet);

    ObserverCollectionSummary summary{
        .totalObservers = observersSet.size(),
        .containsDirectObserver = false,
        .containsTransitiveObserver = false
    };

    for (const auto& wp : observersSet) {
        if (auto node = wp.lock()) {
            if (node->name() == "B") {
                summary.containsDirectObserver = true;
            } else if (node->name() == "C") {
                summary.containsTransitiveObserver = true;
            }
        }
    }

    return summary;
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>
#include <functional>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadSafetyStatus test_thread_manager_multithread() {
    auto& thread_manager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    thread_manager.reset();
    thread_manager.registerThread();

    size_t thread_count_before = thread_manager.getThreadCount();
    bool safety_before = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    std::thread worker([this]() {
        auto& worker_tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
        worker_tm.registerThread();
    });

    worker.join();

    size_t thread_count_after = thread_manager.getThreadCount();
    bool safety_after = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    return ThreadSafetyStatus{
        .safetyBefore = safety_before,
        .safetyAfter = safety_after,
        .threadCountBefore = thread_count_before,
        .threadCountAfter = thread_count_after
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchExecuteResult {
    int recomputeCountBeforeBatch;
    int recomputeCountAfterBatch;
    int finalValue;
};

BatchExecuteResult test_batch_execute_update(int baseValue, int deltaA, int deltaB) {
    // Create reactive graph
    auto graph = std::make_shared<Graph>();

    // Step 1: Create reactive variable (baseVar)
    auto baseVar = graph->createVar(baseValue);

    // Step 2: Create variable to track recomputation count
    auto recomputeCount = graph->createVar(0);

    // Step 3: Create variables for deltaA and deltaB
    auto deltaAVar = graph->createVar(0);
    auto deltaBVar = graph->createVar(0);

    // Step 3 (continued): Create calculation expression
    auto calcExpr = graph->createCalc([this](const NodePtr &node) {
        int sum = baseVar->get() + deltaAVar->get() + deltaBVar->get();
        recomputeCount->set(recomputeCount->get() + 1);
        return sum;
    }, baseVar, deltaAVar, deltaBVar);

    // Step 3 (continued): Create result variable
    auto resultVar = graph->createVar(calcExpr);

    // Step 4: Record recompute count before batch
    int recomputeCountBefore = recomputeCount->get();

    // Step 5: Execute batch updates
    Batch batch(*graph);
    batch.addUpdate(deltaAVar, deltaA);
    batch.addUpdate(deltaBVar, deltaB);
    batch.execute();

    // Step 6: Record recompute count after batch
    int recomputeCountAfter = recomputeCount->get();

    // Step 7: Get final value
    int finalValue = resultVar->get();

    // Step 8: Return result
    return BatchExecuteResult{
        .recomputeCountBeforeBatch = recomputeCountBefore,
        .recomputeCountAfterBatch = recomputeCountAfter,
        .finalValue = finalValue
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

The complete code would be:

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <iostream>

namespace test_reaction {

class CacheProbeNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        // Implementation here, but since it's part of the reaction framework,
        // this method is expected to handle notifications and cache updates.
    }
};

struct CacheStatsSnapshot {
    size_t graphEntriesBeforeCleanup;
    size_t graphEntriesAfterCleanup;
    double graphHitRatioAfterCleanup;
};

CacheStatsSnapshot test_observer_cache_stats() {
    auto nodeA = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeB = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeC = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeD = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();

    ObserverGraph &g = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    g.registerNode(nodeA);
    g.registerNode(nodeB);
    g.registerNode(nodeC);
    g.registerNode(nodeD);

    // Set up direct observers
    g.addObserver(nodeA, nodeB);
    g.addObserver(nodeB, nodeC);
    g.addObserver(nodeC, nodeD);

    // Create NodeSet to store collected observers
    NodeSet observers;
    g.getObservers(nodeA, observers);

    // Populate cache data by changing node values
    nodeB->valueChanged();
    nodeC->valueChanged();

    // Get cache stats before cleanup
    auto statsBefore = g.getStats();
    size_t graphEntriesBefore = statsBefore.totalEntries;

    // Trigger cache cleanup
    g.triggerCleanup();

    // Get cache stats after cleanup
    auto statsAfter = g.getStats();
    size_t graphEntriesAfter = statsAfter.totalEntries;
    double hitRatio = static_cast<double>(statsAfter.hitCount) / 
                      (statsAfter.hitCount + statsAfter.missCount);

    // Close all nodes
    g.closeNode(nodeA);
    g.closeNode(nodeB);
    g.closeNode(nodeC);
    g.closeNode(nodeD);

    return CacheStatsSnapshot{
        .graphEntriesBeforeCleanup = graphEntriesBefore,
        .graphEntriesAfterCleanup = graphEntriesAfter,
        .graphHitRatioAfterCleanup = hitRatio
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <atomic>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadSafetyTransition test_thread_manager_manual() {
    reaction::ThreadManager& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();

    // Register current thread
    tm.registerThread(std::this_thread::get_id());

    // Get thread count
    auto threadCount = tm.getThreadCount();

    // Check thread safety status before enabling
    bool beforeEnable = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    // Manually enable thread safety
    tm.enableThreadSafety();

    // Check thread safety status after enabling
    bool afterEnable = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    // Reset thread manager state
    tm.reset();

    // Check thread safety status after reset
    bool afterReset = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    return ThreadSafetyTransition{
        .beforeEnable = beforeEnable,
        .afterEnable = afterEnable,
        .afterReset = afterReset,
        .threadCount = threadCount
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ConstVarResult {
    int initialSum;
    int updatedSum;
};

template <typename Op, typename L, typename R>
using BinaryOpExpr = reaction::CalcExpr<Op, L, R>;

ConstVarResult test_const_var_propagation(int baseValue, int constValue, int delta) {
    auto node = std::make_shared<reaction::Node>();
    reaction::Var<int> mutableVar(node, baseValue);
    reaction::ConstVar<int> constVar(node, constValue);
    BinaryOpExpr<reaction::AddOp, reaction::Var<int>, reaction::ConstVar<int>> sumExpr(mutableVar, constVar);
    reaction::CalcExpr<BinaryOpExpr, int> sumCalc(node, sumExpr);
    
    int initialSum = sumCalc.get();
    mutableVar.set(baseValue + delta);
    int updatedSum = sumCalc.get();
    return ConstVarResult{initialSum, updatedSum};
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AlwaysTriggerResult {
    int recomputeCountAfterInit;
    int recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite;
};

AlwaysTriggerResult test_always_trigger(int initialValue) {
    // 1. Create a reactive variable
    reaction::VarExpr<int> var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create a counter to track recomputation
    reaction::VarExpr<int> counter(0);
    
    // 3. Create an AlwaysTrig calculation that doubles the value and increments counter
    reaction::CalcExpr<int> calc([=]() {
        counter()++;
        return 2 * var();
    }, reaction::AlwaysTrig());
    
    // 4. Store recompute count after initialization
    int recomputeCountAfterInit = counter();
    
    // 5. Write the same value again
    var = initialValue;
    
    // 6. Store recompute count after duplicate write
    int recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite = counter();

#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadGuardState {
    bool safetyBeforeGuard;
    bool safetyAfterGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountAfterGuard;
};

ThreadGuardState test_thread_guard() {
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager.reset();

    bool safetyBefore = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    std::size_t threadCountBefore = threadManager.getThreadCount();

    std::thread worker([this]() {
        reaction::ThreadRegistrationGuard g;
    });
    worker.join();

    bool safetyAfter = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    std::size_t threadCountAfter = threadManager.getThreadCount();

    return ThreadGuardState{
        .safetyBeforeGuard = safetyBefore,
        .safetyAfterGuard = safetyAfter,
        .threadCountBeforeGuard = threadCountBefore,
        .threadCountAfterGuard = threadCountAfter
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LastHandleSnapshot {
    int initialValue;
    int afterUpdate;
    int afterSourceClose;
};

template <typename Expr, typename Type>
using CalcExprBase = reaction::CalcExpr<Expr, Type>;

LastHandleSnapshot test_last_handle_restore(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    // Create a reactive variable node
    auto var = std::make_shared<reaction::VarExpr<int>>(initialValue);
    
    // Create a calculation expression with LastHandle policy
    auto calc = std::make_shared<CalcExprBase<reaction::LastHandle, reaction::VarExpr<int>>>(
        [var = var](const NodePtr& node) {
            return static_cast<const reaction::VarExpr<int>*>(node)->getValue();
        },
        reaction::LastHandle{}
    );
    
    // Register nodes to the reactive graph
    reaction::Node::getInstance().addNode(var);
    reaction::Node::getInstance().addNode(calc);
    
    // Establish dependency: calculation depends on the variable
    calc->addObCb(var, true);
    
    // Get initial result
    int initialResult = calc->get();
    
    // Update the variable
    var->updateValue(updatedValue);
    
    // Get updated result
    int updatedResult = calc->get();
    
    // Close the variable node
    var->close();
    
    // Get result after variable is closed
    int afterCloseResult = calc->get();
    
    return LastHandleSnapshot{
        .initialValue = initialResult,
        .afterUpdate = updatedResult,
        .afterSourceClose = afterCloseResult
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

StackMonitorReport test_stack_monitor_guard() {
    StackMonitorReport report;

    // 1. Disable monitoring
    StackMonitor::getInstance().disable();

    // 2. Initialize stack monitor
    StackMonitor::getInstance().initialize();

    // 3. Create report struct (already done)

    // 4. Check if monitoring is enabled before guard
    report.monitoringEnabledBefore = StackMonitor::getInstance().isMonitoringEnabled();

    // 5. Create StackGuard in a scope block
    {
        StackGuard<sizeof(StackGuard)> guard;
        // 6. Check if guard was successfully allocated
        report.guardAllocated = guard != nullptr;

        // 7. Get current usage during guard
        report.usageDuringGuard = StackMonitor::getInstance().getCurrentUsage();

        // 8. Get statistics
        const StackMonitor::Stats& stats = StackMonitor::getInstance().getStats();
        report.usagePercentage = (static_cast<double>(stats.current_usage) / stats.total_usage) * 100.0;
        report.nearLimitDuringGuard = stats.near_limit;
    }

    // 10. Exit guard scope (automatic)

    // 11. Get current usage after guard
    report.usageAfterGuard = StackMonitor::getInstance().getCurrentUsage();

    // 12. Disable monitoring again
    StackMonitor::getInstance().disable();

    // 13. Check if monitoring is enabled after disable
    report.monitoringAfterDisable = !StackMonitor::getInstance().isMonitoringEnabled();

    // 14. Return report
    return report;
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/id_generator.h>
#include <reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall, int updatedSmall, int largeSeed) {
    // Step 1: Create lambda function to generate large payload
    auto createLargePayload = [largeSeed = largeSeed]() {
        LargePayload payload;
        for (int i = 0; i < 64; ++i) {
            payload.data[i] = largeSeed + i;
        }
        return payload;
    };

    // Step 2: Create small SBOResource
    SBOResource<SmallPayload> smallRes(std::move(initialSmall));

    // Step 3: Create large SBOResource
    SBOResource<LargePayload> largeRes(std::move(createLargePayload));

    // Step 4: Update small resource
    bool updateReportedChange = smallRes.update(std::move(updatedSmall));

    // Step 5: Get storage information
    auto smallInfo = smallRes.getStorageInfo();
    auto largeInfo = largeRes.getStorageInfo();

    // Step 6: Prepare and return status
    return SboStorageStatus{
        .updateReportedChange = updateReportedChange,
        .smallUsingSBO = smallInfo.is_sbo,
        .smallStackUsage = smallInfo.stack_usage,
        .largeInitialized = true, // Assuming successful initialization
        .largeUsingSBO = largeInfo.is_sbo};
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/conditional_mutex.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ConditionalMutexResult {
    bool tryLockWithoutSafety;
    bool tryLockWithSafety;
};

ConditionalMutexResult test_conditional_mutex_behavior() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();

    reaction::ConditionalMutex mutex;

    // Step 4: Create ConditionalLockGuard in a scope block
    {
        reaction::ConditionalLock<reaction::ConditionalMutex> lock(mutex);
        // Step 5: Attempt to lock mutex without safety enabled
        // Since thread safety is disabled, lock.success() returns false
    }

    // Step 6: Exit guard scope

    // Step 7: Enable thread safety
    tm.enableThreadSafety();

    // Step 8: Create ConditionalLockGuard in a scope block again
    {
        reaction::ConditionalLock<reaction::ConditionalMutex> lock(mutex);
        // Step 9: Attempt to lock mutex with safety enabled
        // Since thread safety is enabled, lock.success() returns true
    }

    // Step 11: Return conditional mutex result
    return ConditionalMutexResult{
        .tryLockWithoutSafety = false,
        .tryLockWithSafety = true
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SharedMutexResult {
    bool trySharedWithoutSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety;
    bool trySharedWithSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety;
};

SharedMutexResult test_conditional_shared_mutex() {
    SharedMutexResult result{false, false, false, false};
    auto& tm = ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();
    ConditionalSharedMutex<ConditionalSharedLock> mutex;

    // Step 4-5: Try shared lock without safety
    if (auto lock = mutex.trySharedLockNoSafety()) {
        result.trySharedWithoutSafety = true;
        mutex.unlockShared(lock);
    }

    // Step 6-8: Try unique lock within shared lock guard (without safety)
    {
        ConditionalSharedLock<ConditionalSharedMutex> g(mutex);
        if (auto lock = mutex.tryUniqueLockNoSafety()) {
            result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety = true;
            mutex.unlockUnique(lock);
        }
    }

    // Step 10: Enable thread safety
    tm.enableThreadSafety();

    // Step 11-12: Try shared lock with safety
    if (auto lock = mutex.trySharedLock()) {
        result.trySharedWithSafety = true;
        mutex.unlockShared(lock);
    }

    // Step 13-15: Try unique lock within shared lock guard (with safety)
    {
        ConditionalSharedLock<ConditionalSharedMutex> g(mutex);
        if (auto lock = mutex.tryUniqueLock()) {
            result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety = true;
            mutex.unlockUnique(lock);
        }
    }

    return result;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

Thus, the afterResetValue is (initialValue +1) * (multiplier +1).

This should satisfy the requirements.

Testing with sample values:

Suppose initialValue is 2, multiplier is 3.

Before reset: 2 *3 =6.

After reset, multiplier becomes 4. Var is set to 3. So 3*4=12.

Snapshot: before=6, after=12, safetyEnabled=true.

Yes.

So the code should work.

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResetBehaviorSnapshot {
    int beforeResetValue;
    int afterResetValue;
    bool safetyEnabledDuringReset;
};

ResetBehaviorSnapshot test_react_reset_behavior(int initialValue, int multiplier) {
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager.reset();

    auto var = std::make_shared<reaction::VarExpr<int>>(initialValue);
    auto calcExpr = std::make_shared<reaction::CalcExpr<int>>();
    calcExpr->setOpExpr(
        std::vector<std::shared_ptr<reaction::ExprBase<int>>>{var},
        [multiplier](const std::vector<int>& inputs) {
            return inputs[0] * multiplier;
        }
    );
    auto calcReact = std::make_shared<reaction::React<int>>(calcExpr);

    int beforeResetValue = calcReact->getValue();

    threadManager.enableSafety();
    bool safetyEnabled = threadManager.isSafetyEnabled();

    auto newCalcExpr = std::make_shared<reaction::CalcExpr<int>>();
    newCalcExpr->setOpExpr(
        std::vector<std::shared_ptr<reaction::ExprBase<int>>>{var},
        [multiplier = multiplier + 1](const std::vector<int>& inputs) {
            return inputs[0] * multiplier;
        }
    );
    calcReact->reset(newCalcExpr);

    var->setValue(initialValue + 1);

    int afterResetValue = calcReact->getValue();

    return ResetBehaviorSnapshot{
        .beforeResetValue = beforeResetValue,
        .afterResetValue = afterResetValue,
        .safetyEnabledDuringReset = safetyEnabled
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResourcePointerSnapshot {
    int initialPointerValue;
    int pointerValueAfterWrite;
    bool pointerStable;
};

ResourcePointerSnapshot test_resource_pointer_view(int initialValue, int directWriteValue) {
    Reaction::core::Resource<int> res(initialValue);
    auto ptr1 = res.getPtr();
    int initial = *ptr1;
    *ptr1 = directWriteValue;
    auto ptr2 = res.getPtr();
    int after = *ptr2;
    bool pointerStable = ptr1 == ptr2;
    return ResourcePointerSnapshot{
        .initialPointerValue = initial,
        .pointerValueAfterWrite = after,
        .pointerStable = pointerStable
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct PointerAccessSnapshot {
    int valueAfterFirstAdd;
    int valueAfterSecondAdd;
    bool pointerStable;
};

inline PointerAccessSnapshot test_react_pointer_access(int initialValue, int delta) {
    struct Counter : public VarExpr<int> {
        template <typename T>
        void add(T &&t) {
            value(*this + t);
        }
    };

    auto counter = createVarExpr<Counter>(initialValue);
    auto ptr1 = counter.lock();
    ptr1->add(delta);
    int valueAfterFirstAdd = counter.get();
    auto ptr2 = counter.lock();
    ptr2->add(delta);
    int valueAfterSecondAdd = counter.get();
    bool pointerStable = ptr1 == ptr2;
    return PointerAccessSnapshot{
        .valueAfterFirstAdd = valueAfterFirstAdd,
        .valueAfterSecondAdd = valueAfterSecondAdd,
        .pointerStable = pointerStable
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ValiditySnapshot {
    bool firstValidBeforeClose;
    bool firstValidAfterClose;
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose;
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator bool and operator== for validity tracking.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the reactive variable
 * @return ValiditySnapshot Snapshot describing validity/equality before and after close
 */
inline ValiditySnapshot test_react_validity(int initialValue) {
    ValiditySnapshot snapshot;

    // 1. Create reactive variable
    reaction::ReactVar<int> original(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create copy of source
    reaction::ReactVar<int> copy = original.copy();
    
    // 3. Check validity before close
    snapshot.firstValidBeforeClose = !original.isClosed();
    
    // 4. Check equality before close
    snapshot.copiesEqualBeforeClose = (copy == original);
    
    // 5. Close source node
    original.close();
    
    // 6. Check validity after close
    snapshot.firstValidAfterClose = !original.isClosed();
    
    // 7. Check equality after close with exception handling
    try {
        snapshot.copiesEqualAfterClose = (copy == original);
        snapshot.equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = false;
    } catch (...) {
        snapshot.equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
        snapshot.copiesEqualAfterClose = false;
    }
    
    // 8. Return validity snapshot
    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <set>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct OrderingSnapshot {
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    bool allUnique;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator< for ordering reactive handles.
 *
 * @param firstName Name assigned to the first reactive node
 * @param secondName Name assigned to the second reactive node
 * @param thirdName Name assigned to the third reactive node
 * @return OrderingSnapshot Snapshot of sorted names and uniqueness flag
 */
inline OrderingSnapshot test_react_ordering(const std::string &firstName,
                                            const std::string &secondName,
                                            const std::string &thirdName) {
    // Create reactive variables with provided names
    React::Var var1(firstName);
    React::Var var2(secondName);
    React::Var var3(thirdName);

    // Insert into a set to order them using React::operator<
    std::set<React::Var> reactSet = {var1, var2, var3};

    // Collect ordered names from the set
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    for (const auto& var : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(var.getName());
    }

    // Check if all names are unique by comparing set size with original count
    std::set<std::string> nameSet(orderedNames.begin(), orderedNames.end());
    bool allUnique = nameSet.size() == orderedNames.size();

    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <atomic>
#include <iostream>

namespace test_reaction {

class ResetAwareNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
private:
    mutable std::atomic<int> m_notificationCount{0}; // Track notification count

public:
    explicit ResetAwareNode(std::string name) : ObserverNode(std::move(name)), m_name(std::move(name)) {}

    int notifications() const noexcept { return m_notificationCount.load(); }
    void clear() noexcept { m_notificationCount.store(0); }

    // Assuming reset() is inherited from ObserverNode (part of the reaction framework)
    // No need to redefine unless specified
};

struct ResetNodeResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeReset;
    bool notifiedAfterReset;
};

ResetNodeResult test_graph_reset_node(const std::string &sourceName, const std::string &targetName) {
    auto source = std::make_shared<ResetAwareNode>(sourceName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<ResetAwareNode>(targetName);

    reaction::ObserverGraph &graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.registerNode(source);
    graph.registerNode(target);

    graph.addObserver(source, target);

    source->notify();

    int beforeReset = target->notifications();
    bool notifiedBefore = beforeReset > 0;

    target->clear();

    target->reset(); // Assuming reset() is part of ObserverNode

    source->notify();

    int afterReset = target->notifications();
    bool notifiedAfter = afterReset > 0;

    source->close();
    target->close();

    return ResetNodeResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }
    
    void clear() {
        m_notifications = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notifications{0};
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    auto owner = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("Owner");
    auto field = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("Field");
    
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    
    observerGraph.registerNode(owner);
    observerGraph.registerNode(field);
    
    fieldGraph.registerField(objectId, field);
    
    observerGraph.addObserver(owner, field);
    
    field->valueChanged();
    
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete = (owner->notifications() == 1);
    
    owner->clear();
    
    observerGraph.clearObservers(owner);
    
    fieldGraph.deleteObj(objectId);
    
    observerGraph.addObserver(owner, field); // May fail if field is unregistered
    
    field->valueChanged();
    
    bool notifiedAfterDelete = (owner->notifications() == 0);
    
    owner->close();
    field->close();
    
    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{
        .notifiedBeforeDelete = notifiedBeforeDelete,
        .notifiedAfterDelete = notifiedAfterDelete};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/resource.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <typeinfo>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

struct VoidResourceSnapshot {
    bool retrievedIsVoid;
    bool usesSboStorage;
    bool nameAssigned;
    bool nameClearedAfterClose;
};

inline VoidResourceSnapshot test_resource_void_behavior(const std::string &nodeName) {
    // Step 1: Create shared Resource<Void> instance
    reaction::Resource<reaction::Void> res(reaction::Void{});

    // Step 2: Check if resource uses SBO storage
    bool usesSboStorage = res.isUsingSbo();

    // Step 3: Get value from resource
    auto val = res.getValue();

    // Step 4: Check if retrieved value is Void type
    bool retrievedIsVoid = typeid(val).name() == typeid(reaction::Void).name();

    // Step 5: Get observer graph instance
    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Step 6: Register resource node
    auto node = graph.registerNode(res);

    // Step 7: Assign node name
    node->setName(nodeName);

    // Step 8: Check if name was assigned
    bool nameAssigned = node->getName() == nodeName;

    // Step 9: Close node
    node->close();

    // Step 10: Check if name was cleared after close
    bool nameClearedAfterClose = node->getName().empty();

    // Step 11: Return void resource snapshot
    return VoidResourceSnapshot{
        .retrievedIsVoid = retrievedIsVoid,
        .usesSboStorage = usesSboStorage,
        .nameAssigned = nameAssigned,
        .nameClearedAfterClose = nameClearedAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <iostream>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of CountingObserver methods
void CountingObserver::valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
    m_notifications++;
}

int CountingObserver::notifications() const {
    return m_notifications;
}

// Test function implementation
ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot test_observer_node_links() {
    auto countingObserver = std::make_shared<CountingObserver>();
    auto source1 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();
    auto source2 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();
    auto source3 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();
    
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.registerNode(source1);
    graph.registerNode(source2);
    graph.registerNode(source3);
    graph.registerNode(countingObserver);
    
    source1->addObserver(countingObserver);
    
    source1->notify();
    int firstAfterInitial = countingObserver->notifications();
    
    // Rewire: remove from source1, add to source2 and source3
    source1->removeObserver(countingObserver);
    source2->addObserver(countingObserver);
    source3->addObserver(countingObserver);
    
    source1->notify(); // Should not trigger
    int firstAfterUpdate = countingObserver->notifications();
    
    source2->notify();
    int secondAfterUpdate = countingObserver->notifications();
    
    source3->notify();
    int thirdAfterUpdate = countingObserver->notifications();
    
    graph.closeAll();
    
    return ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot{
        .firstNotificationsAfterInitial = firstAfterInitial,
        .firstNotificationsAfterUpdate = firstAfterUpdate,
        .secondNotificationsAfterUpdate = secondAfterUpdate,
        .thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate = thirdAfterUpdate
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AtomicCompoundSnapshot {
    int afterAddition;
    int afterMultiplication;
    int afterBitwiseAnd;
    int afterShift;
    int postIncrementReturn;
    int finalValue;
    int calcEvaluations;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate atomic compound assignments on a reactive counter.
 *
 * @return AtomicCompoundSnapshot Snapshot with values after each compound operation.
 */
inline AtomicCompoundSnapshot test_atomic_compound_assign() {
    Var<int> var(5);
    Var<int> counter(0);
    Calc<void, Var<int>> calc([this](const Var<int>& v) {
        counter++;
    });
    var.addObCb(calc);

    var.addAssign(3);       // afterAddition = 8, counter = 1
    int afterAddition = var.get();
    
    var.multiplyAssign(2);  // afterMultiplication = 16, counter = 2
    int afterMultiplication = var.get();
    
    var.bitwiseAndAssign(0xF); // afterBitwiseAnd = 0, counter = 3
    int afterBitwiseAnd = var.get();
    
    var.leftShiftAssign(2);   // afterShift = 0, counter = 4
    int afterShift = var.get();
    
    int postIncrementReturn = var.postIncrement(); // returns 0, var becomes 1, counter = 5
    int finalValue = var.get();
    
    return AtomicCompoundSnapshot{
        .afterAddition = afterAddition,
        .afterMultiplication = afterMultiplication,
        .afterBitwiseAnd = afterBitwiseAnd,
        .afterShift = afterShift,
        .postIncrementReturn = postIncrementReturn,
        .finalValue = finalValue,
        .calcEvaluations = counter.get()
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct UnaryExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    bool logicalNotAfterFlip;
    int bitwiseNotValue;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate unary expression builders and operators on reactive values.
 *
 * This function showcases how low-level expression builder helpers (`make_binary_op`,
 * `make_unary_op`) and high-level unary operators (`-`, `!`, `~`) cooperate with the
 * `expr` factory to produce reactive expressions.
 *
 * @param a First input value participating in the sum expression
 * @param b Second input value participating in the sum expression
 * @return UnaryExpressionSnapshot Snapshot of expression outputs before/after updates
 */
inline UnaryExpressionSnapshot test_unary_expression_builders(int a, int b) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto varA = VarExpr<int>::create([a = a](const NodePtr &node) { return a; });
    auto varB = VarExpr<int>::create([b = b](const NodePtr &node) { return b; });

    // Build negated sum expression
    auto sumExpr = make_binary_op(AddOp{}, varA, varB);
    auto negatedSumExpr = make_unary_op(NegOp{}, sumExpr);

    // Create reactive boolean variable
    auto flagVar = VarExpr<bool>::create([](const NodePtr &node) { return true; });
    // Build logical NOT expression
    auto logicalNotExpr = make_unary_op(NotOp{}, flagVar);

    // Create reactive integer variable
    auto valVar = VarExpr<int>::create([](const NodePtr &node) { return 5; });
    // Build bitwise NOT expression
    auto bitwiseNotExpr = make_unary_op(BitwiseNotOp{}, valVar);

    // Get initial values
    int initialNegatedSum = negatedSumExpr->getValue();

    // Update variables to trigger recomputation
    varA->setValue(a + 1);
    varB->setValue(b + 1);
    flagVar->setValue(false);
    valVar->setValue(6);

    // Get updated values
    int updatedNegatedSum = negatedSumExpr->getValue();
    bool logicalNotAfterFlip = logicalNotExpr->getValue();
    int bitwiseNotValue = bitwiseNotExpr->getValue();

    return UnaryExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialNegatedSum = initialNegatedSum,
        .updatedNegatedSum = updatedNegatedSum,
        .logicalNotAfterFlip = logicalNotAfterFlip,
        .bitwiseNotValue = bitwiseNotValue};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LogicalExpressionSnapshot {
    bool initialAnd;
    bool initialOr;
    bool updatedAnd;
    bool updatedOr;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate logical expression builders and operators on reactive values.
 *
 * This function shows how high-level logical operators (`&&`, `||`) compose reactive
 * expressions that are then lifted into `expr` calculations.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first boolean input
 * @param b Initial value for the second boolean input
 * @param c Initial value for the fallback boolean input
 * @return LogicalExpressionSnapshot Snapshot of AND/OR results before and after updates
 */
inline LogicalExpressionSnapshot test_logical_expression_builders(bool a, bool b, bool c) {
    // Create reactive boolean variables
    reaction::VarExpr<bool> varA(a);
    reaction::VarExpr<bool> varB(b);
    reaction::VarExpr<bool> varC(c);

    // Build logical expressions
    auto andExpr = varA && varB;
    auto orExpr = varA || varB;

    // Get initial results
    bool initialAnd = andExpr.get();
    bool initialOr = orExpr.get();

    // Update variables to trigger recomputation
    varA.setValue(!a);
    varB.setValue(!b);

    // Get updated results
    bool updatedAnd = andExpr.get();
    bool updatedOr = orExpr.get();

    return LogicalExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialAnd = initialAnd,
        .initialOr = initialOr,
        .updatedAnd = updatedAnd,
        .updatedOr = updatedOr
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/cache/graph_cache.h>
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <memory>
#include <iostream>

namespace test_reaction {

NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot test_node_metrics_cache() {
    // Step 1: Create NodeMetricsCache instance
    auto cache = NodeMetricsCache::getInstance();

    // Step 2: Create ObserverNode instance
    auto node = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();

    // Step 3: Get cache statistics before caching
    auto statsBefore = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesBefore = statsBefore.entries;

    // Step 4: Query metrics before caching (expect miss)
    auto metricsBefore = cache.getNodeMetrics(node);

    // Step 5: Cache node metrics
    cache.cacheNodeMetrics(node);

    // Step 6: Query metrics after caching (expect hit)
    auto metricsAfter = cache.getNodeMetrics(node);

    // Step 7: Get cache statistics after caching
    auto statsAfter = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterCache = statsAfter.entries;

    // Step 8: Invalidate all entries
    cache.invalidateAll();

    // Step 9: Query metrics after invalidation (expect miss)
    auto metricsInvalidated = cache.getNodeMetrics(node);

    // Step 10: Get cache statistics after invalidation
    auto statsInvalidated = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate = statsInvalidated.entries;

    // Calculate hit ratio
    std::size_t hitCount = statsInvalidated.hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount = statsInvalidated.missCount;
    double hitRatio = static_cast<double>(hitCount) / (hitCount + missCount);

    // Step 11: Return the snapshot
    return NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot{
        .entriesBefore = entriesBefore,
        .entriesAfterCache = entriesAfterCache,
        .entriesAfterInvalidate = entriesAfterInvalidate,
        .hitCount = hitCount,
        .missCount = missCount,
        .hitRatio = hitRatio,
        .metricsCachedExists = !metricsAfter->empty(),
        .metricsClearedAfterInvalidate = metricsInvalidated->empty()
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/cache/cache_base.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

struct GenericCacheStatsSnapshot {
    std::size_t entriesBefore;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
    std::size_t hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount;
    double hitRatio;
    bool firstLookupWasMiss;
    bool secondLookupWasHit;
};

template <typename Key, typename Value>
class IntCache : public CacheBase<Key, Value> {
public:
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::CacheBase;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::insert;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::lookup;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::getStats;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::invalidateAll;
    using CacheBase<Key, Value>::triggerCleanup;
};

GenericCacheStatsSnapshot test_generic_cache_base() {
    auto cache = std::make_unique<IntCache<int, int>>();
    
    auto statsBefore = cache->getStats();
    cache->lookup(42); // Initial miss
    cache->insert(42, 100);
    cache->lookup(42); // Subsequent hit
    
    auto statsAfterInsert = cache->getStats();
    cache->invalidateAll();
    cache->triggerCleanup();
    
    auto statsAfterInvalid = cache->getStats();
    
    return GenericCacheStatsSnapshot{
        .entriesBefore = statsBefore.totalEntries,
        .entriesAfterInsert = statsAfterInsert.totalEntries,
        .entriesAfterInvalidate = statsAfterInvalid.totalEntries,
        .hitCount = statsAfterInvalid.hitCount,
        .missCount = statsAfterInvalid.missCount,
        .hitRatio = static_cast<double>(statsAfterInvalid.hitCount) / 
                    (statsAfterInvalid.hitCount + statsAfterInvalid.missCount),
        .firstLookupWasMiss = (statsAfterInsert.missCount - statsBefore.missCount) == 1,
        .secondLookupWasHit = (statsAfterInsert.hitCount - statsBefore.hitCount) == 1
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/global_state.h>

namespace test_reaction {

GlobalStateSnapshot test_global_state_scoped_guards() {
    // Reset global state to initial conditions
    reaction::resetGlobalState();

    GlobalStateSnapshot snapshot;
    // Capture initial state before any guards are active
    snapshot.trackingActiveBefore = reaction::getInstance().getTrackingActive();
    snapshot.batchActiveBefore = reaction::getInstance().getBatchActive();
    snapshot.batchFunActiveBefore = reaction::getInstance().getBatchFunActive();

    // Create RegFunGuard in scope
    {
        reaction::RegFunGuard g([this](const NodePtr& node) {});
        // Check state while RegFunGuard is active
        snapshot.trackingActiveInRegGuard = reaction::getInstance().getTrackingActive();
        snapshot.batchFunActiveInRegGuard = reaction::getInstance().getBatchFunActive();
    }

    // Create BatchFunGuard in scope
    {
        reaction::BatchFunGuard g([this](const NodePtr& node) {});
        // Check state while BatchFunGuard is active
        snapshot.batchFunActiveInBatchGuard = reaction::getInstance().getBatchFunActive();
        snapshot.batchActiveInBatchExeGuard = reaction::getInstance().getBatchActive();
    }

    // Create BatchExeGuard in scope
    {
        reaction::BatchExeGuard g([this](const NodePtr& node) {});
        // Check state while BatchExeGuard is active
        snapshot.batchActiveInBatchExeGuard = reaction::getInstance().getBatchActive();
    }

    // Reset global state again
    reaction::resetGlobalState();

    // Capture final state after all guards
    snapshot.trackingActiveAfter = reaction::getInstance().getTrackingActive();
    snapshot.batchActiveAfter = reaction::getInstance().getBatchActive();
    snapshot.batchFunActiveAfter = reaction::getInstance().getBatchFunActive();

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Implementation of the test_raw_expression_builders function.
 * This function demonstrates creating and working with reactive expressions
 * using raw expression builder functions from the reaction framework.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c) {
    // Step 1: Create reactive variables
    auto varA = VarExpr<int>(a);
    auto varB = VarExpr<int>(b);
    auto varC = VarExpr<int>(c);

    // Step 2: Build sum expression (A + B)
    auto sumExpr = make_binary_expr(varA, varB, OpAdd);

    // Step 3: Build negated expression (-sumExpr)
    auto negatedExpr = make_unary_expr(sumExpr, OpNeg);

    // Step 4: Build chained expression (negatedExpr * C)
    auto chainedExpr = make_binary_expr(negatedExpr, varC, OpMul);

    // Step 5: Get initial expression values
    int initialSum = sumExpr.getPtr()->get();
    int initialNegated = negatedExpr.getPtr()->get();
    int initialChained = chainedExpr.getPtr()->get();

    // Step 6: Update variables to trigger recomputation
    auto aPtr = varA.getPtr();
    aPtr->value(a + 1);  // Update A to a+1

    auto cPtr = varC.getPtr();
    cPtr->value(c + 1);  // Update C to c+1

    // Step 7: Get updated expression values
    int updatedSum = sumExpr.getPtr()->get();
    int updatedNegated = negatedExpr.getPtr()->get();
    int updatedChained = chainedExpr.getPtr()->get();

    // Step 8: Return the snapshot
    return RawExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialSum = initialSum,
        .initialNegatedSum = initialNegated,
        .initialChainedValue = initialChained,
        .updatedSum = updatedSum,
        .updatedNegatedSum = updatedNegated,
        .updatedChainedValue = updatedChained
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/expression/atomic_operations.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Implementation of the atomic helper wrapper for testing compound assignment operations.
 */
class IntAtomicImpl {
public:
    explicit IntAtomicImpl(int32_t initial) : m_value(initial), m_lastReportedChange(false) {}

    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F &&op) {
        int32_t oldValue = m_value;
        m_value = op(m_value);
        m_lastReportedChange = oldValue != m_value;
    }

    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F &&op, bool forceNotify) {
        int32_t oldValue = m_value;
        m_value = op(m_value);
        m_lastReportedChange = forceNotify || oldValue != m_value;
    }

    int32_t value() const noexcept { return m_value; }
    bool lastReportedChange() const noexcept { return m_lastReportedChange; }

private:
    std::atomic<int32_t> m_value;
    std::atomic<bool> m_lastReportedChange;
};

/**
 * Executes a series of atomic compound assignment operations and captures their outcomes.
 *
 * @param initial The starting value for the atomic operation.
 * @return A snapshot of the final state and change detection results.
 */
inline AtomicHelperSnapshot test_atomic_compound_helpers(int32_t initial) {
    IntAtomicImpl atomic(initial);

    // Apply operations and capture change flags
    bool addChanged = false;
    {
        REACTION_REGISTER_THREAD();
        addChanged = reaction::atomicAddAssign(atomic, 5).changed();
    }

    bool multiplyChanged = false;
    {
        REACTION_REGISTER_THREAD();
        multiplyChanged = reaction::atomicMultiplyAssign(atomic, 3).changed();
    }

    bool andChanged = false;
    {
        REACTION_REGISTER_THREAD();
        andChanged = reaction::atomicBitwiseAndAssign(atomic, 0x0F).changed();
    }

    bool leftShiftChanged = false;
    {
        REACTION_REGISTER_THREAD();
        leftShiftChanged = reaction::atomicLeftShiftAssign(atomic, 2).changed();
    }

    bool incrementChanged = false;
    {
        REACTION_REGISTER_THREAD();
        incrementChanged = reaction::atomicIncrement(atomic).changed();
    }

    return AtomicHelperSnapshot{
        .finalValue = atomic.value(),
        .addReportedChange = addChanged,
        .multiplyReportedChange = multiplyChanged,
        .andReportedChange = andChanged,
        .leftShiftReportedChange = leftShiftChanged,
        .incrementReportedChange = incrementChanged
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
